visual_attributes.morphology: flowing air mass descending a slope
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: can cover large geographic areas, from mountain slopes to valleys
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: descending from elevated areas to lower regions

domain_specific_visual.temperature_gradient: cooler at higher elevations, warmer at lower elevations
domain_specific_visual.density_variation: higher density at the top, lower density at the bottom

domain_specific_functional.GRAVITATIONAL_FORCE: drives the movement of the air mass
domain_specific_functional.RADIATIVE_COOLING: initiates the cooling of the air mass

relationships.description: Katabatic winds are commonly found blowing from the ice sheets of Antarctica.
Piteraq winds in Greenland are a type of katabatic wind.
Strong katabatic winds can exacerbate wildfires by providing oxygen and spreading flames.

contextual_attributes.origin: geographic regions with elevated terrains and ice sheets
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Antarctica, Greenland, California, Japan, New Zealand, Spain
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: influences local climate and weather patterns, impacts human activities
